decisions.what: Enhanced testimonials section with complete social proof implementation
decisions.why: Task specification required replacing testimonials placeholder with full social proof section including 4 testimonial cards with avatar placeholders, trust indicators with updated statistics (15+ Years, 2,500+ Acres, 50+ Partnerships, 98% Satisfaction), and credibility elements (certifications, awards, industry recognition)

decisions.what: Implemented comprehensive CSS styles for social proof components
decisions.why: Added styles for .social-proof-section, .testimonials-grid, .testimonial-card with professional appearance, .testimonial-quote with decorative quotation marks, .testimonial-author with avatar styling, .trust-indicators with gradient background, .stat-item with hover effects, and .credibility-elements grid layout

decisions.what: Used semantic HTML with blockquote elements for testimonials
decisions.why: Specification explicitly required using blockquote elements for testimonials and proper heading hierarchy for accessibility and semantic correctness

decisions.what: Added avatar placeholders using SVG icons
decisions.why: Specification requested avatar placeholders for testimonial authors - implemented with circular SVG icon containers for visual consistency

decisions.what: Implemented responsive grid layouts using CSS Grid
decisions.why: Used CSS Grid with auto-fit and minmax for responsive testimonial cards, trust statistics, and credibility items to ensure proper layout across all device sizes

decisions.what: Applied CSS custom properties throughout component styles
decisions.why: Maintained design system coherence by referencing existing CSS variables for colors, spacing, typography, and shadows defined in base.css
